tldr: Alex, you're running 21 live ads but most of the weight is sitting on a handful of pboy hat carousels and four hero t-shirt creatives that have been live for 160+ days — well past your 21-day refresh window. The bigger pattern: your headline field is doing almost no work because it's just repeating the product title, so the body copy (which is where your voice actually lives) is shouldering the entire hook.

benchmark.context: Typical graphic apparel brands refresh hero creative every ~21 days; the top quartile lands around 14. You have four ads past day 160, which means your practical window has already collapsed — the ones still working are masking the ones that died weeks ago.

ads.headline: born to swim ocean is a t -shirt
ads.body: helps you drown if you don't take it off before swimming
drivers: Live for 160 days — more than 7× your refresh window for an apparel brand at your scale
Headline points at the 'born to swim' shirt but the landing URL goes to the 'i lift to forget' product page — that mismatch tanks post-click intent and confuses the algorithm
Body copy 'helps you drown if you don't take it off before swimming' is duplicated verbatim across three other live ads, so Andromeda is clustering them as one concept

ads.headline: born to swim ocean is a t -shirt
ads.body: "Be free my brothers! Go forth and live your little fishy lives. I love you all!"
drivers: Running unchanged since December — 160 days deep on a creative meant to last ~21
Same product/headline pairing exists in a younger 17-day variant, which means this evergreen version is competing against its own clone for delivery
Headline is just the product title — it's burning the strongest real estate on the ad on something the image already shows

ads.headline: the entire bog is laughing at you t -shirt
drivers: 160 days running with zero copy variation — the body line and headline are identical to a 17-day refresh you already shipped
Shipping the same exact ad twice tells the algorithm there's one concept here, not two — you're paying for variant testing and getting cluster suppression instead
Headline is the raw product title with a stray space ('t -shirt') — looks like a CSV export, not an ad

ads.headline: i must amuse - t-shirt
ads.body: helps you drown if you don't take it off before swimming
drivers: 160 days live AND the body copy belongs to a different shirt — 'helps you drown' is the born-to-swim line, not the i must amuse line
That copy/product mismatch repeats across multiple ads, suggesting a feed-mapping issue in whatever's pushing creative into Meta
Already cloned at day 15 with identical text, so you're double-paying for the same fatigued concept

ads.headline: life is fun and nice and i love to smile - t-shirt
drivers: 160 days running — by far past the point where Andromeda has stopped surfacing this in fresh auctions
'Cool smells in every purchase' is the only ad on the account using a sensory-promise hook — losing this voice when it dies is a real loss
No headline work is happening — the headline field is the product title verbatim, so the body is the entire hook

ads_compact.headline: dogecore
ads_compact.body: helping you be the worst version of yourself since 2011

ads_compact.headline: dissociation and dehydration - t-shirt
ads_compact.body: t shirts to play co-op games alone in

ads_compact.headline: ̤̮⚘.✧˖° new friend with every purchase・ﻌ・  ̤̮⚘.✧˖
ads_compact.body: worldwide delivery and easy returns at dogecore.com •ᴗ• ̤̮☻✧

hero_concept.hook: shirts for people who are mostly fine
hero_concept.headline: kill god. respectfully.
hero_concept.primary_text:
your funniest copy is buried in the body field. the headline on almost every live ad is just the product title with a typo'd space — that's the most-read line on the ad doing zero work.

this one flips it. headline carries the joke, body carries the product, image carries the deadpan. same voice you already write in, just put where people actually read it.

worldwide shipping. small very polite dog not actually included.
hero_concept.visual_direction: The 'kill god' tee laid flat on a slightly wrinkled pastel bedsheet, shot top-down with a half-eaten piece of toast and a melting popsicle next to it — deadpan internet-core still life.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Every live ad uses the headline field as a product title dump, which means the voice that makes this brand work ('helping you be the worst version of yourself since 2011', 'cool smells in every purchase') is hidden in body copy where most people scroll past. This concept moves the punchline up and gives Andromeda a genuinely new concept signature against the 18 carousels currently clustering as one.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a flat-lay on a slightly wrinkled pale lavender bedsheet with a half-eaten piece of buttered toast on a chipped ceramic plate to one side and a half-melted blue popsicle dripping onto a paper towel on the other, with lighting from soft overcast daylight through a north-facing window producing low-contrast even illumination with gentle natural shadows. Deadpan internet-core still life aesthetic — slightly messy, intimate, lived-in, the kind of thing you'd see on a Tumblr post in 2014, lo-fi but sharply focused on the garment. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL — preserve the exact deep black colorway of the cotton jersey t-shirt AND every printed graphic, color, and text element exactly as shown on the chest of the reference image — strictly NO color shift, NO removing of print, NO altering of graphic colors, NO converting black to charcoal or grey, NO warming the tone. STRUCTURE — preserve the exact construction: crew neckline, short sleeves, regular straight unisex t-shirt fit, garment laid flat with sleeves spread naturally. PRESERVE the front-chest 'kill god' printed graphic exactly — every letter, every line, all colors, same placement and scale as in the reference. Do NOT remove or modify the chest print, do NOT alter the typography, do NOT change neckline shape or sleeve length, do NOT add pockets, do NOT add buttons or zippers, do NOT change the silhouette to fitted or oversized. Render the product's fabric, color, finish, weave, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, neckline, sleeves, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the fabric type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove any hardware or stitching detail, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself — text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: Four ads past day 160 — they're already dead weight in the auction.
next_step.headline: Walk through the dogecore refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Alex, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep — work with us or don't.

method_note: Built from your public Meta Ad Library only — no account access, no private metrics, scored against 2025–2026 Andromeda clustering norms and apparel refresh windows.
